0.name: Potential

states.balanced: Pure presence — open, available, ready. Beginner's mind. Every moment fresh
states.tooMuch: Hovering in endless possibility, never landing. All potential, no actualization
states.tooLittle: Past wounds have sealed the opening. Creating from constriction
states.unacknowledged: Operating reactively without seeing options — doors invisible

0.extended: The seed state of all becoming. Not naivety but infinite potential — the moment before choice collapses possibility into actuality. Contains everything, committed to nothing yet.

1.name: Will

states.balanced: Conscious direction aligned with Now — choosing from clarity, not compulsion
states.tooMuch: Future-projected control — manipulation, forcing outcomes, white-knuckling reality
states.tooLittle: Past-anchored defeat — passivity, "what's the point," learned helplessness
states.unacknowledged: Direction without awareness of directing — drifting with momentum, confusing habit for choice

1.extended: The capacity to direct attention and energy toward chosen ends. Not force but focused intention — the ability to say 'this, not that' and mean it.

2.name: Wisdom

states.balanced: Direct knowing that proves accurate — comfort with not-knowing; perceptions land without argument
states.tooMuch: Information hoarding, analysis paralysis — the veil becomes a wall
states.tooLittle: Chronic confusion, lost faith in perception — seeking external validation for what you already know
states.unacknowledged: Perceptions that prove accurate but surprise you — insights shape decisions you believe are random

2.extended: Deep knowing that precedes analysis. The capacity to recognize what matters before understanding why. Wisdom isn't accumulated knowledge — it's seeing through to essence.

3.name: Nurturing

states.balanced: Growth happening without forcing — care that doesn't exhaust; holding space without clutching
states.tooMuch: Controlling under the guise of care — smothering, martyrdom, boundary dissolution
states.tooLittle: Chronic creative block, nothing grows — emotional unavailability disguised as independence
states.unacknowledged: Nurturing roles you never chose — others experience you as caretaker while you feel depleted

3.extended: Tending what needs to develop. Creating conditions for growth without forcing outcomes. The patient attention that allows emergence.

4.name: Order

states.balanced: Structure that serves what it organizes — clear, reliable; things make sense in your presence
states.tooMuch: Rigid over-organization, control — mistaking the throne for the kingdom
states.tooLittle: Chronic disorganization — can't prioritize, can't sequence; the pieces never assemble
states.unacknowledged: Organizing without knowing you're organizing — others rely on your structure while you feel formless

4.extended: The impulse to organize chaos into workable form. Not rigid control but functional architecture — creating containers that allow complexity to be navigated.

5.name: Culture

states.balanced: Living transmission of meaning — teaching that adapts; tradition that breathes; belonging without conformity
states.tooMuch: Rigid orthodoxy, dogma — conformity demanded; the letter kills the spirit; windows sealed
states.tooLittle: Rootless, no shared framework — every generation reinventing the wheel; lonely intelligence
states.unacknowledged: Transmitting without knowing it — you're already the teacher; you just don't see it

5.extended: The transmission of meaning across time and between people. Rituals, languages, practices that carry wisdom forward. Living connection to what has proven valuable.

6.name: Compassion

states.balanced: Felt recognition that honors both self and other — connection without fusion
states.tooMuch: Emotional fusion, boundary dissolution — can't tell where you end and others begin
states.tooLittle: Emotional numbness, disconnection — relationships stay surface
states.unacknowledged: Deep connection disguised as indifference — others feel your compassion while you feel nothing

6.extended: The capacity to feel with rather than just about. Genuine alignment between beings that recognizes shared experience. Connection that honors difference.

7.name: Drive

states.balanced: Movement from feeling — direction from genuine engagement
states.tooMuch: Driving past the destination — momentum that can't stop
states.tooLittle: Stalled at the threshold — feeling without forward motion
states.unacknowledged: Momentum you don't know you have — or stagnation you can't see

7.extended: Energy channeled toward destination. Not aggression but focused motion — the capacity to maintain direction through resistance without being consumed.

8.name: Fortitude

states.balanced: Holding without forcing — capacity that contains rather than crushes
states.tooMuch: Gripping past necessity — strength becoming rigidity
states.tooLittle: Collapse under pressure — holding feels too dangerous
states.unacknowledged: Strength or collapse you don't see in yourself

8.extended: The ability to bear what must be borne. Not hardness but resilient strength — capacity to hold form under pressure without breaking or becoming rigid.

9.name: Discipline

states.balanced: Practice flows without force — solitude connects rather than isolates; refinement serves growth, not anxiety
states.tooMuch: Practice becomes perfectionism — isolation feels necessary rather than chosen; refining compulsively, fearing what happens if you stop
states.tooLittle: Past experiences have made practice feel dangerous — chronic inability to sustain cultivation
states.unacknowledged: You don't know you're practicing — or refusing to. Expertise arrives 'without trying'

9.extended: Solitary cultivation of capacity through repetition. The hermit's lamp illuminates through sustained attention, not flash. Intention transformed into embodied skill.

10.name: Source

states.balanced: You recognize yourself as the turning — each moment is fresh entry. Present-moment awareness as natural state
states.tooMuch: The Wheel seems to turn you — fate replaces choice; patterns repeat without awareness; external locus of control
states.tooLittle: Trying to step off the Wheel — spiritual bypassing, rejecting manifestation, clinging to fixed states
states.unacknowledged: You don't know you're on the Wheel — surprise at obvious consequences; unconscious patterns cycling

10.extended: The turning point where what was outside enters the system. Not fate but timing — recognizing what's arriving and how to meet it.

11.name: Equity

states.balanced: Fairness flows naturally — clear sense of what's right without obsessive calculation; give and receive in flowing exchange
states.tooMuch: Fairness becomes accounting — obsessive tracking of who owes what; relationships reduced to exchange calculations
states.tooLittle: Chronic inability to claim what's owed — tolerance of unfair treatment; past experiences have made fairness feel dangerous
states.unacknowledged: You don't know you're weighing — tallying grievances you didn't know you were counting

11.extended: Right relationship made manifest. Not punishment but recalibration — adjusting what's out of proportion until balance returns.

12.name: Sacrifice

states.balanced: Release flows naturally from completion — surrender as fulfillment, not defeat
states.tooMuch: Releasing before completion — martyrdom, premature surrender, performative self-denial
states.tooLittle: Inability to let go — exhaustion from chronic holding, controlling grip
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing you've already surrendered — or that you haven't

12.extended: Conscious letting go of what no longer serves. Not loss but clearing — creating space by releasing grip. Revealing value hidden by habitual grasping.

13.name: Change

states.balanced: Transformation flows from felt necessity — endings honored, renewal trusted
states.tooMuch: Constant upheaval — ending things before they've lived, fear disguised as liberation
states.tooLittle: Clinging to what's dead — stagnation disguised as loyalty, fear of all endings
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing what's dying in you — or that anything is

13.extended: The completion that allows new beginning. Not destruction but transformation — the moment when what was must fully end for what will be to emerge.

14.name: Balance

states.balanced: Equilibrium flows from genuine integration — centered presence holding complexity
states.tooMuch: Premature harmony — conflict avoidance, suppressing necessary distinctions
states.tooLittle: Chronic fragmentation — inability to find center, scattered by every engagement
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing how you're integrating — or that you aren't

14.extended: Continuous adjustment that maintains flow. Not static middle but active calibration — sensing when to add, when to subtract, how to blend.

15.name: Abstraction

states.balanced: Conceptual depth flows from genuine understanding — seeing patterns that liberate
states.tooMuch: Over-intellectualization — pattern-seeing that becomes paranoia, analysis without end
states.tooLittle: Avoidance of depth — literalism, refusal of symbol, willful naivety
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing how patterns bind you — or that they do

15.extended: Recognition of pattern stripped of content. Not temptation but clarity about mechanism — seeing how things work beneath surface appearance.

16.name: Breakthrough

states.balanced: Destruction flows from genuine insight — structures fall that needed to fall
states.tooMuch: Premature shattering — chaos disguised as liberation, unable to let anything stand
states.tooLittle: Clinging to dead structures — maintaining the Tower past its usefulness
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing what's about to fall — or that it should

16.extended: Rapid dissolution of what couldn't hold. Not catastrophe but liberation — structures built on false premises collapse when truth arrives.

17.name: Inspiration

states.balanced: Radiance flows from genuine development — shining because what was cultivated reached completion
states.tooMuch: Chronic ungrounded optimism — projecting light forward in anxiety, dazzling rather than illuminating
states.tooLittle: Spiritual exhaustion — cultivation that never flowers, hope defeated by experience
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing you're radiating — or that you've stopped

17.extended: The draw toward what's genuinely desired. Not fantasy but orientation — knowing which star to steer by. Recognized, not given; remembered, not arrived.

18.name: Imagination

states.balanced: Vision flows from genuine integration — dreaming because experience has been fully processed
states.tooMuch: Chronic illusion — confusing dream with reality, lost in symbolic depth
states.tooLittle: Spiritual literalism — refusing depth, distrusting intuition, cutting off the symbolic
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing what you're dreaming — or that dreams are shaping your life

18.extended: Capacity to move through uncertainty without forcing clarity. The moon illuminates enough to walk, not enough to see everything. Trust the path that reveals itself.

19.name: Actualization

states.balanced: Radiance without ego — shining because you ARE light, not to prove it
states.tooMuch: Solar inflation — mistaking the ray for the source, blinding others
states.tooLittle: Solar eclipse — dimming what has been actualized, false modesty
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing you're shining — or that you've stopped

19.extended: Full expression of inherent nature. Not achievement but authenticity — allowing what you are to shine without dimming or distortion.

20.name: Awareness

states.balanced: Recognition without finality — knowing that continues to deepen
states.tooMuch: Rigid judgement — recognition becoming condemnation, spiritual arrogance
states.tooLittle: Spiritual sleep — refusing the call, perpetual seeking that never lands
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing that you know — or that you're refusing to know

20.extended: The capacity to perceive from outside the system while remaining within it. Not judgment but recognition — seeing what's true across all layers.

21.name: Creation

states.balanced: Completion without finality — recognizing the cycle closes while remaining open to new cycles
states.tooMuch: Premature completion — claiming arrival before the journey is done
states.tooLittle: Perpetual incompletion — refusing to let cycles close, endless seeking
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing that cycles have completed — or haven't

21.extended: The point of departure that is also arrival. Full integration that allows release into next becoming. Completion that opens rather than closes.

22.name: Activation
22.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
22.description: Beginning of pure potential through Intent. The spark before direction.
22.extended: Potential (0) expressed through Fire at the threshold of emergence. This is the moment undifferentiated possibility first catches fire — not yet pointed anywhere, not yet committed, but no longer dormant. The Ace is always the parent archetype at its most nascent: here, infinite possibility meets the Intent channel's demand for direction and produces the raw spark. Gestalt Seed through Fire: the whole self igniting before it knows what for. Inner bound — what Potential IS at rest before it moves. When this appears as a transient, something is trying to begin but hasn't found its shape yet.

states.balanced: Pure presence — open, available, ready. Beginner's mind. Every moment fresh
states.tooMuch: Hovering in endless possibility, never landing. All potential, no actualization
states.tooLittle: Past wounds have sealed the opening. Creating from constriction
states.unacknowledged: Operating reactively without seeing options — doors invisible

23.name: Orientation
23.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
23.description: Decisive direction through aspiration. Choosing which star to steer by.
23.extended: Inspiration (17) expressed through Fire at the formative scale. The Star archetype gives aspirational pull — knowing which light to move toward. Through the Intent channel, this becomes decisive orientation: choosing a direction not from logic but from vision. Spirit Fruition through Fire: the mature harvest of inner knowing expressed as purposeful pointing. Number 2 is Balanced/Decisive — the inner face of the Spirit pair, where choice hasn't yet multiplied into abundance. This is direction-setting, not direction-defending. When this appears as a transient, the question isn't what to do but which calling to follow.

states.balanced: Radiance flows from genuine development — shining because what was cultivated reached completion
states.tooMuch: Chronic ungrounded optimism — projecting light forward in anxiety, dazzling rather than illuminating
states.tooLittle: Spiritual exhaustion — cultivation that never flowers, hope defeated by experience
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing you're radiating — or that you've stopped

24.name: Assertion
24.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
24.description: Primal claim of structure through Intent. Expansive declaration outward.
24.extended: Order (4) expressed through Fire at the formative scale. The Emperor archetype organizes chaos into structure; through Intent, this becomes the assertive declaration of pattern outward into the world. Mind Seed through Fire: the first impulse to impose structure as a willed act. Number 3 is Primal/Expansive — the inner face of the Mind pair, where structure is broadcasting outward before it deepens. Assertion doesn't just establish order internally, it claims territory. When this appears as a transient, there's an urge to organize, to declare, to establish framework through forceful clarity.

states.balanced: Structure that serves what it organizes — clear, reliable; things make sense in your presence
states.tooMuch: Rigid over-organization, control — mistaking the throne for the kingdom
states.tooLittle: Chronic disorganization — can't prioritize, can't sequence; the pieces never assemble
states.unacknowledged: Organizing without knowing you're organizing — others rely on your structure while you feel formless

25.name: Alignment
25.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
25.description: Peaceful momentum, stable drive. Intent finding its natural rhythm.
25.extended: Drive (7) expressed through Fire at the operative scale. The Chariot archetype channels emotional momentum toward goals; through Intent, this becomes aligned action — will and feeling moving in the same direction. Emotion Medium through Fire: the sustained transmission of motivated energy. Number 4 is Peace/Stillness — the inner face of the Emotion pair, where Drive has found its groove before it needs to defend it. The four wands form a gateway, a celebration of momentum at rest in its own rhythm. When this appears as a transient, effort feels natural rather than forced; the doing is itself the reward.

states.balanced: Movement from feeling — direction from genuine engagement
states.tooMuch: Driving past the destination — momentum that can't stop
states.tooLittle: Stalled at the threshold — feeling without forward motion
states.unacknowledged: Momentum you don't know you have — or stagnation you can't see

26.name: Dedication
26.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
26.description: Internal practical release through Intent. Testing commitment through engagement.
26.extended: Sacrifice (12) expressed through Fire at the operative scale. The Hanged Man archetype releases what no longer serves; through Intent, this becomes dedication tested through friction. Body Feedback through Fire: the integration that comes from letting go of the wrong approach and finding the right one through active engagement. Number 5 is Internal/Personal — the inner face of the Body pair, where the testing is private and personal. The five wands crossing are not public combat but internal clarification, burning away assumptions to reveal what you're actually committed to. When this appears as a transient, dedication is being pressure-tested behind closed doors.

states.tooMuch: Releasing before completion — martyrdom, premature surrender, performative self-denial
states.tooLittle: Inability to let go — exhaustion from chronic holding, controlling grip

27.name: Recognition
27.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
27.description: External acknowledgment of sacrifice. Intent visible and honored.
27.extended: Sacrifice (12) expressed through Fire at the operative scale. Same parent as Dedication (26) but at the outer face: where Dedication tests commitment internally, Recognition is commitment made visible and witnessed by others. Body Feedback through Fire: the cycle completes when what was sacrificed for is publicly honored. Number 6 is External/Shared — the outer face of the Body pair, where private effort becomes social acknowledgment. The victory parade, the laurel wreath — not ego gratification but the natural consequence of sustained release becoming visible achievement. When this appears as a transient, what was quietly given up is now seen and valued by the group.

states.tooLittle: Inability to let go — exhaustion from chronic holding, controlling grip
states.unacknowledged: Not knowing you've already surrendered — or that you haven't

28.name: Resolve
28.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
28.description: Gathered determination, harvested momentum. Defending chosen direction.
28.extended: Drive (7) expressed through Fire at the operative scale. Same parent as Alignment (25) but at the outer face: where Alignment finds natural rhythm (inner/peaceful), Resolve defends it against resistance (outer/gathering). Emotion Medium through Fire: the sustained transmission of motivated energy now facing opposition. Number 7 is Gathering/Reaping — the outer face of the Emotion pair, where all that was sown must be harvested or defended. Holding ground requires gathering everything that was scattered and directing it at the single point of defense. When this appears as a transient, something worth protecting is under pressure and the question is whether the momentum holds.

states.tooMuch: Driving past the destination — momentum that can't stop
states.tooLittle: Stalled at the threshold — feeling without forward motion
states.unacknowledged: Momentum you don't know you have — or stagnation you can't see

29.name: Command
29.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
29.description: Deep authority, threshold structure. Intent moving with unstoppable clarity.
29.extended: Order (4) expressed through Fire at the completive scale. Same parent as Assertion (24) but at the outer face: where Assertion declares structure outward (inner/primal), Command IS structure in motion — authority so complete it doesn't need to assert (outer/profound). Mind Seed through Fire: the initiating impulse of mental organization now operating at its deepest threshold. Number 8 is Unfathomable/Deep — the outer face of the Mind pair, where accumulated pattern achieves self-sustaining momentum. When this appears as a transient, decisions move with clarity and speed because the structural understanding is total.

states.balanced: Structure that serves what it organizes — clear, reliable; things make sense in your presence
states.tooMuch: Rigid over-organization, control — mistaking the throne for the kingdom
states.tooLittle: Chronic disorganization — can't prioritize, can't sequence; the pieces never assemble
states.unacknowledged: Organizing without knowing you're organizing — others rely on your structure while you feel formless

30.name: Resilience
30.operationNote: FLAG: own-operation semantics for bounds/agents not yet ruled; parentOperation provided
30.description: Abundant persistence through aspiration. Strength from continued hoping.
30.extended: Inspiration (17) expressed through Fire at the completive scale. Same parent as Orientation (23) but at the outer face: where Orientation chose which star to follow (inner/decisive), Resilience is still following it after everything that's happened (outer/abundant).
